blast_radius.recommendation: Proceed — file count and weight are manageable; key integration risk is the 16b prerequisite gate, not the blast radius itself.

one_way_doors.note: The plan's proposed ACTION_SHORTCUTS list drops 'Open graph view' (Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+G) and 'Export YAML' (Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+Y), which currently exist in ShortcutsHelp.tsx lines 11-12 and are pinned by ShortcutsHelp.test.tsx lines 17-31. These shortcuts are real, registered in App.tsx, and tested. The plan's replacement implementation silently omits them. The existing test that asserts graph/YAML shortcuts exist WILL FAIL after the replacement unless the plan's implementation is corrected.
one_way_doors.mitigation_in_plan: NO — plan's ACTION_SHORTCUTS array does not include these entries; will cause regression in existing ShortcutsHelp.test.tsx

one_way_doors.change: Alt+1-4 inspector-tab shortcut added to ACTION_SHORTCUTS
one_way_doors.note: The plan's proposed ACTION_SHORTCUTS includes '{ keys: "Alt+1-4", action: "Switch inspector tab (Spec/Graph/YAML/Runs)" }'. The existing ShortcutsHelp.test.tsx at line 33-38 explicitly asserts this shortcut does NOT appear ('does not list retired inspector tab shortcuts'). The plan would cause this existing test to fail.
one_way_doors.mitigation_in_plan: NO — this entry was explicitly removed from the live component in a prior commit; re-adding it will break an existing test that guards against it

findings.title: ShortcutsHelp replacement silently drops two live shortcuts and re-adds one retired one
findings.detail: The plan's Task 4 Step 3 proposes a full replacement of ShortcutsHelp.tsx. The proposed ACTION_SHORTCUTS array is missing 'Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+G' (Open graph view) and 'Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+Y' (Export YAML), both of which: (1) exist in the current ShortcutsHelp.tsx (lines 11-12), (2) are tested by ShortcutsHelp.test.tsx lines 17-31, and (3) have working keyboard handlers registered in App.tsx. The replacement will cause those two existing tests to fail. Additionally the proposed array adds 'Alt+1-4' / 'Switch inspector tab (Spec/Graph/YAML/Runs)', which is a retired shortcut that ShortcutsHelp.test.tsx line 33-38 explicitly asserts must NOT appear. This is a triple regression baked into the plan's literal code.
evidence: src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/components/common/ShortcutsHelp.tsx:11-12 — Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+G and Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+Y entries
src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/components/common/ShortcutsHelp.test.tsx:17-31 — test that pins graph/YAML shortcuts
src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/components/common/ShortcutsHelp.test.tsx:33-38 — test that asserts Alt+1 inspector shortcut is absent
16c plan Task 4 Step 3 — ACTION_SHORTCUTS array omits both live shortcuts, includes the retired Alt+1-4 entry
findings.fix: Add 'Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+G' and 'Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+Y' to ACTION_SHORTCUTS (or to REFERENCE_SHORTCUTS if they are reference-class actions). Remove the 'Alt+1-4 / Switch inspector tab' entry. Update ShortcutsHelp.test.tsx to add the new subheading assertions while preserving the existing graph/YAML assertions.

findings.title: 16b primitives do not exist in the current codebase — plan treats them as already landed
findings.detail: The plan's Task 1 (FilterChipStrip) imports 'lookupAuditCharacteristic' from './auditCharacteristics' and uses 'DataTrustTier' from '@/types/index'. Task 3 imports AuditCharacteristicIcon and TrustTierBadge from 16b. Neither exists yet: the catalog directory contains only CatalogDrawer.tsx, CatalogDrawer.test.tsx, PluginCard.tsx, and PluginCard.test.tsx. The types/index.ts PluginSummary interface (line 277-282) has no capability_tags, audit_characteristics, data_trust_tier, or usage_when_to_use fields. The plan correctly names 16b as a prerequisite but TDD Step 2 ('run test to verify it fails') for Task 1 will fail with a module-not-found error, not a component-not-found error, which is a different failure mode than the plan predicts. This matters because an agent executing the plan may misread the failure mode as environment breakage and stop.
evidence: src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/types/index.ts:277-282 — PluginSummary has no new fields
find /home/john/elspeth/src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/components/catalog — only 4 files exist, no auditCharacteristics.ts, no AuditCharacteristicIcon, no TrustTierBadge
16c Task 1 Step 3 — imports lookupAuditCharacteristic from './auditCharacteristics'
16c Task 3 Step 3 — imports DataTrustTier from '@/types/index'
findings.fix: Add a preflight gate at the top of the plan: before any task begins, verify that 16b has landed by checking for the existence of auditCharacteristics.ts, AuditCharacteristicIcon.tsx, TrustTierBadge.tsx in the catalog directory, and that DataTrustTier is exported from types/index.ts. If absent, block 16c and land 16b first. The plan already states this dependency but does not make it mechanically enforceable.

findings.title: Existing CatalogDrawer.test.tsx mock fixtures use the pre-16a PluginSummary shape — the new tests will conflict
findings.detail: The plan's Task 3 Step 1 adds new tests to CatalogDrawer.test.tsx using an extended fixture that includes capability_tags, audit_characteristics, data_trust_tier, and the four usage_when* prose fields. The existing CatalogDrawer.test.tsx (lines 7-25) uses fixtures with only {name, plugin_type, description, config_fields}. When the tests are merged into one file with a shared vi.mock that returns only the old shape, the new tests' references to fields like plugin.capability_tags will dereference undefined, producing silent empty arrays. The plan claims 'all existing tests still pass' but this is only true if the mock override in the new describe block fully supersedes the file-level mock — which it does via beforeEach + vi.mocked().mockResolvedValue(). However, the existing tests' mock fixtures (lines 7-25) do NOT include the new fields, and now that matchesFilters and scorePlugin read capability_tags and audit_characteristics from every plugin, empty-field plugins will still pass matchesFilters (because filters are empty sets by default), so existing tests pass. This is fine — but document it so the next reader understands the invariant.
evidence: src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/components/catalog/CatalogDrawer.test.tsx:7-25 — old mock fixtures lack new fields
16c Task 3 Step 3 matchesFilters — when capabilityTags.size === 0, all plugins pass; old fixtures are safe
findings.fix: This is actually not a blocker once understood — the empty-filter pass-through is correct. Downgrade to MAJOR, noted for documentation. However, the agent should know: if any future test activates a filter chip and then checks the old-shape fixtures, the undefined capability_tags dereference will throw at runtime (plugin.capability_tags.some(...) on undefined). The fixture shape in the file-level mock should be updated to include the new fields (as empty arrays/null) to maintain forward compatibility.

findings.title: scorePlugin extension ignores example_use — asymmetric prose search coverage
findings.detail: The plan extends scorePlugin to match across usage_when_to_use, usage_when_not_to_use, and capability_tags. It omits example_use, which is a 16a field with the same prose-search value as the other two prose fields. The omission is not explained and creates asymmetry: an operator who writes a canonical example containing domain vocabulary (the CSV plugin's example_use will contain 'government web pages' from the canonical test case) won't find it by typing that phrase. This is a minor functional gap but it sets a pattern that could silently stay incorrect as more plugins author their example_use fields.
evidence: 16c Task 3 Step 3, scorePlugin extension — target includes usage_when_to_use and usage_when_not_to_use but not example_use
16a spec — example_use: str | None is a first-class metadata field, and the canonical test case uses it
findings.fix: Add plugin.example_use ?? '' to the target array in scorePlugin. No architecture change required — one line.

findings.title: availableCapabilityTags derived from allPluginsForTab (pre-filter) but filter chips always reflect the pre-filter universe
findings.detail: The availableCapabilityTags, availableTrustTiers, and availableAuditCharacteristics memos are derived from allPluginsForTab (the unfiltered tab list). This means if the user activates a 'csv' tag filter, narrowing the visible list to the csv plugin, the chip strip still shows all other tags from the full tab list (e.g. 'network', 'blob'). Clicking 'network' while 'csv' is active would produce an empty list (AND of non-overlapping sets). This is a UX 'filter into empty state' trap that is not flagged in the plan's risk table. The plan's test suite does not cover this. It is not a crash, but it is a confusing interaction that will surface in the Task 5 manual smoke.
evidence: 16c Task 3 Step 3 — availableCapabilityTags memo iterates allPluginsForTab, not pluginList
16c Task 3 Step 1 — no test exercises a second filter activation when the first has already narrowed the list
findings.fix: Document this as known behaviour in the risk table (it is a standard multi-filter UX tradeoff — filter chips showing the full universe vs. contextually narrowed options). Alternatively, derive available tags from pluginList (post-filter) for each non-empty group, but this changes the semantics. Either choice is defensible; the plan should make it explicit.

findings.title: counts memo uses filtersByTab but scorePlugin does not see per-tab filters — tab badge counts can mismatch visible list counts
findings.detail: The counts memo in Task 3 Step 3 calls matchesFilters(p, filtersByTab[tab]) and scorePlugin(query, p) to compute badge counts. The scorePlugin call inside counts uses the same function as the pluginList memo, so they are consistent. However, the scorePlugin function in counts uses the same extended prose-search target as the one in pluginList only if Task 3's scorePlugin extension is applied before the counts memo reads it. Since both are in the same file and scorePlugin is a module-level function, this is fine. No actual mismatch risk — but the counts memo's scorePlugin call in its anonymous arrow function does not guard against the new fields being undefined (plugin.capability_tags.join(' ') will throw if capability_tags is undefined). This affects old-shape fixtures if a filter is activated.
evidence: 16c Task 3 Step 3 counts memo — scorePlugin(query, p) call, where p may be old-shape (no capability_tags) if the file-level mock isn't updated
16c Task 3 Step 3 scorePlugin extension — plugin.capability_tags.join(' ') with no null guard
findings.fix: Add a null guard: plugin.capability_tags?.join(' ') ?? '' in scorePlugin. This is idiomatic for a field that may be absent on old API responses (progressive enhancement). Alternatively, assert the field is always present (since 16a made it required), which is cleaner but requires the file-level mock to be updated.

findings.title: InlineChatSourceEntry ARIA structure: role=region with aria-label on a div — semantically unusual
findings.detail: The plan wraps InlineChatSourceEntry in <div role='region' aria-label='Inline data from chat'>. Landmark regions are appropriate for large content areas; a single catalog row (card-like element) is not a landmark. Using role=region means screen readers will announce 'Inline data from chat, region' as a navigation landmark, which is heavier than intended for a single-row affordance. The existing PluginCard uses no landmark role on its outer div — it uses role='button' only on the interactive disclosure header. The synthetic entry should follow the same pattern for structural consistency.
evidence: 16c Task 2 Step 3 — <div role='region' aria-label='Inline data from chat'>
src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/components/catalog/PluginCard.tsx:146 — outer div has no landmark role
findings.fix: Remove role='region' from the outer div. The aria-label on a generic div without a landmark role has no effect, which is the correct outcome — the component's visual distinction (class name) and the 'Try it' button's aria-label are sufficient for accessibility. If screen reader discoverability of the synthetic entry is a concern, the existing catalog list container's context provides it.

findings.title: CatalogDrawer 'empty state' message does not account for filters — text will read 'No plugins matching X' even when the query is empty but filters are active
findings.detail: The existing empty-state branch in CatalogDrawer's render reads: searchQuery.trim() ? 'No plugins matching ...' : 'No plugins available.' With filter chips added, a user can activate a filter with no search query, narrow to zero results, and see 'No plugins available.' (misleading — there are plugins, the filter excluded them). The plan's Task 3 does not update this empty-state message.
evidence: src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/components/catalog/CatalogDrawer.tsx:341-344 — empty state message logic
16c Task 3 — no mention of updating the empty state message for filter-active-no-results case
findings.fix: Add a tertiary condition: if pluginList.length === 0 and anyFilterActive (check filters state), show 'No plugins match the active filters.' instead of 'No plugins available.'

findings.title: FilterChipStrip imports lookupAuditCharacteristic from './auditCharacteristics' — import path is relative to catalog/
findings.detail: The import path './auditCharacteristics' assumes the file is at catalog/auditCharacteristics.ts (or .tsx). The plan confirms auditCharacteristics is a 16b primitive in the catalog directory. This is consistent with the file structure section. No issue architecturally, just noting that the relative import creates a coupling between FilterChipStrip and the catalog directory — if auditCharacteristics is ever extracted to a shared location, both files would need updating.
evidence: 16c Task 1 Step 3 — import { lookupAuditCharacteristic } from './auditCharacteristics'
findings.fix: No action required. If auditCharacteristics is later promoted to a shared utility, update both import sites.

findings.title: scorePlugin sort order: plan inherits lower-is-better semantics, correctly preserved
findings.detail: The existing scorePlugin uses lower-score = better-match semantics (score 0 = perfect prefix match). The plan extends the target string but preserves this sort direction (.sort((a, b) => a.score - b.score)). Extending the target string increases absolute score values for partial matches (longer target = larger gaps), which may push some previously-visible results below the MIN_FUZZY_CONFIDENCE threshold. This is the plan's stated mitigation ('long target strings naturally raise the noise floor; this is the existing behaviour'). The statement is correct — confidenceFromScore divides by targetLength, so longer targets are appropriately normalized. No action required.
evidence: src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/utils/fuzzyScore.ts:65-72 — confidenceFromScore normalizes by targetLength
16c Task 3 Step 3 scorePlugin extension — target string concatenation

pattern_alignment.plan_approach: useState for filtersByTab keyed by CatalogTab; derived helpers (filters, setFilters) scoped to active tab
pattern_alignment.project_standard: useState + useMemo throughout CatalogDrawer; no global store for transient drawer state
pattern_alignment.note: Per-tab filter record is a natural extension of the existing searchQuery pattern. The emptyFilters() factory ensures correct initialization without sharing references.

pattern_alignment.pattern: Custom DOM events for cross-component communication
pattern_alignment.plan_approach: InlineChatSourceEntry dispatches PREFILL_CHAT_INPUT_EVENT imported from PluginCard
pattern_alignment.project_standard: PluginCard.tsx exports PREFILL_CHAT_INPUT_EVENT; ChatInput.tsx consumes it (confirmed at ChatInput.tsx:12). Re-using the same event constant is correct.
pattern_alignment.note: Re-use of the existing event constant avoids creating a second event name for the same action. InlineChatSourceEntry's import from PluginCard is a minor coupling that is acceptable given the shared semantic.

pattern_alignment.pattern: Controlled filter state flows unidirectionally down to FilterChipStrip
pattern_alignment.plan_approach: FilterChipStrip is a pure controlled component: filters prop in, onChange callback out. No internal state.
pattern_alignment.project_standard: PluginCard, existing catalog components follow controlled/callback pattern

pattern_alignment.pattern: Synthetic entry distinguishes itself from real plugin entries
pattern_alignment.plan_approach: InlineChatSourceEntry is a separate component with a distinct class name and no plugin data binding
pattern_alignment.project_standard: Design doc 08 explicitly frames it as 'an option, not a plugin'; reconnaissance confirmed no backend plugin
pattern_alignment.note: The category distinction is architecturally clean — InlineChatSourceEntry does not implement any PluginCard interface, has no schema expansion, and is positioned above pluginList.map() in the render tree. The hybrid is modeled honestly.

pattern_alignment.plan_approach: Two static arrays (ACTION_SHORTCUTS, REFERENCE_SHORTCUTS); ShortcutList sub-component factored out
pattern_alignment.project_standard: Current flat SHORTCUTS array with a single dl; the sub-component extraction is a legitimate refactoring step
pattern_alignment.note: The factoring of ShortcutList is clean and testable. The structural test (heading.nextElementSibling) is brittle against element insertion but acceptable for a small static component.

scope_split_assessment.detail: The boundary between 16b (primitives: types, auditCharacteristics table, AuditCharacteristicIcon, TrustTierBadge, PluginCard rewrite) and 16c (integration: FilterChipStrip, InlineChatSourceEntry, CatalogDrawer wiring, ShortcutsHelp regroup) is coherent. 16c does not claim primitive work that belongs to 16b, and 16b does not claim integration work. The plan correctly states that 16c cannot land before 16b. The FilterChipStrip component is integration work (consumes the auditCharacteristics table from 16b) rather than a primitive — the boundary is correct.
scope_split_assessment.note: One mild tension: FilterChipStrip exports CatalogFilters, which is a shared type used by CatalogDrawer. An alternative decomposition would put CatalogFilters in types/index.ts or a shared types file. The current placement (in FilterChipStrip.tsx) makes CatalogDrawer import from a sibling component to get a type, which is a minor coupling. This is not a problem in practice but is worth noting.

complexity_assessment.note: The plan has 4 tasks, each under 5 steps, all integrating into a single drawer. No tracer bullet needed — the integration surface is small and the tests validate end-to-end wiring within each task.

custom_vs_library.custom_code: toggle<T>(set: Set<T>, value: T): Set<T> helper in FilterChipStrip
custom_vs_library.available_library: None in the current stack — this is a 4-line utility, correct to inline

blocking_issues.summary: Task 4 ShortcutsHelp replacement drops two live shortcuts (Ctrl+Shift+G, Ctrl+Shift+Y) and re-adds one retired shortcut (Alt+1-4). Existing tests will fail on both counts. Must be corrected in the plan's proposed code before execution.

16b primitives (auditCharacteristics.ts, DataTrustTier, AuditCharacteristicIcon, TrustTierBadge) do not exist in the codebase. All Tasks 1-3 depend on them. A preflight gate is required. The plan names the dependency but does not make it mechanically fail-safe for an executing agent.

recommendations.summary: Update the file-level CatalogDrawer.test.tsx mock fixture to include the new PluginSummary fields (as empty arrays / null) so old-fixture plugins pass through matchesFilters safely when future tests activate filters.
Add plugin.example_use ?? '' to the scorePlugin target array to complete prose-search coverage across all four 16a prose fields.

Document in the risk table that available chip values reflect the full tab list (not the post-filter list), and that activating conflicting filters will produce an empty state. If this is the intended UX, a comment in CatalogDrawer.tsx explains the design choice for the next reader.
Add null guards to scorePlugin's capability_tags.join() call (plugin.capability_tags?.join(' ') ?? '') to tolerate old-shape API responses or fixtures without crashing.
Remove role='region' from InlineChatSourceEntry's outer div. The landmark role is semantically disproportionate for a single-row affordance and diverges from PluginCard's structural pattern.

Update the empty-state message in CatalogDrawer to distinguish 'no plugins match the active filters' from 'no plugins available' so users understand why the list is empty when filters are active with no search.
